title: Paragraph Writing
sample: Volunteering is very useful for young people. First, it teaches them responsibility because they learn to help others without waiting for money. Also, it helps them develop communication skills by working with different people. For example, students who join a school clean-up campaign learn teamwork and respect for public spaces. For these reasons, volunteering should be encouraged in Moroccan schools.
checklist: My paragraph has one clear idea

title: Essay Writing
sample: Nowadays, technology plays an important role in education. It helps students find information quickly and learn through videos, applications, and online exercises. For instance, a student in a small town can watch lessons and practice English at home. However, technology can also distract learners if they spend too much time on games or social media. Therefore, students should use digital tools wisely. In conclusion, technology is beneficial when it supports learning and does not replace effort.
commonMistakes: Writing one block instead of paragraphs
Starting with a memorized sentence that does not fit the topic
checklist: My conclusion is short and clear

title: Formal Letter
usefulPhrases: I look forward to your reply.
sample:
Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to complain about the lack of clean bins near our school. Many students throw paper and plastic bottles on the ground because there are not enough places for waste. This situation makes the area dirty and unhealthy. I would like to ask the local council to provide more bins and organize an awareness campaign for students.

I look forward to your reply.

Yours faithfully,
A concerned student
commonMistakes: Using Hi in a formal letter
Forgetting the purpose in the first paragraph
checklist: I ended with a suitable closing

title: Informal Letter
usefulPhrases: I am writing to tell you about ...
sample:
Dear Youssef,

How are you? I hope your Bac revision is going well. I am writing to tell you about our new English club. We meet every Friday afternoon and practice speaking, vocabulary, and writing. Last week, we discussed how young people can volunteer in their neighborhoods. I think you should join us because it will help you become more confident in English.

Write back soon.
Best wishes,
Omar
commonMistakes: Being too formal with a close friend
checklist: I used correct greeting and ending

title: Email
sample:
Subject: English Study Group

Dear classmates,

I hope you are well. I am writing about the English study group we discussed yesterday. I suggest that we meet every Wednesday after school in the library. We can review grammar, practice functions, and correct one writing task each week. Please let me know if this time is suitable for you.

Best regards,
Salma
commonMistakes: No subject when the task asks for one
checklist: My email has a clear purpose

title: Article
usefulPhrases: To make a difference, we should ...
outline: End with advice or a call to action
sample:
Protecting Our School Environment

A clean school is not only beautiful; it also helps students feel comfortable and responsible. One major problem is that some students throw paper and plastic bottles in the yard. Another problem is wasting water in bathrooms. To solve this, each class can create a green team to remind students to use bins and save water. The school can also organize a monthly clean-up day. If everyone takes part, our school will become a better place to learn.
checklist: I ended with advice or a conclusion

title: Report
usefulPhrases: The aim of this report is to ...
sample:
Report on Students' Reading Habits

The aim of this report is to describe reading habits among students in our class. Most students said that they read online posts every day, but only a few read books regularly. The main reasons are lack of time and the attraction of social media. It was also found that students prefer short stories and books related to real life. I recommend that the school library organize reading clubs and display interesting books for teenagers.
commonMistakes: Giving personal feelings instead of findings
checklist: My report has a clear title

title: Book Review
structure: Book title and author if known
usefulPhrases: The book tells the story of ...
The message of the book is ...
sample: I recently read a short book about a village girl who wanted to continue her education. The story shows how determination and family support can change a person's life. What I liked most was the main character's courage. She faced many difficulties, but she did not give up her dream. The language was simple and the message was powerful. I recommend this book to students because it reminds us that education is a key to a better future.
checklist: I recommended the book to a reader

title: Film Review
usefulPhrases: I would recommend this film because ...
sample: The film I watched recently is about a group of students who create a small project to help their community. It is a social drama with a positive message. The main characters face problems such as lack of money and discouragement, but they continue working together. The best part was the final scene, where the whole neighborhood supports them. I would recommend this film to young people because it shows the value of teamwork and hope.
commonMistakes: Using only good or bad without explanation
checklist: I named or described the film
I gave reasons for my opinion

title: Opinion Essay
sample: I believe that students should take part in school clubs. The main reason is that clubs help students develop skills that they do not always learn in class, such as teamwork, leadership, and communication. Another reason is that clubs make school life more interesting and reduce stress. For example, an English club can help students speak with confidence before the Bac exam. For these reasons, I think school clubs should be encouraged in all high schools.
commonMistakes: Giving arguments for both sides without control

title: Describing a Person
sample: The person I admire most is my English teacher. She is patient, organized, and encouraging. What makes her special is the way she explains difficult lessons using simple examples from our daily life. She also gives us useful writing practice and corrects our mistakes kindly. Thanks to her support, many students have become more confident in speaking and writing English. I admire her because she shows that a good teacher can change students' lives.
checklist: I explained why the person matters

title: Describing a Place
sample: Chefchaouen is a beautiful city in northern Morocco. It is famous for its blue streets, calm atmosphere, and mountain views. Visitors can walk through the old medina, take photos, buy traditional products, and enjoy local food. What I like most about the city is its peaceful feeling and clean streets. I think it is a wonderful place for people who want to relax and discover Moroccan culture.
checklist: I ended with a feeling or recommendation

title: Argumentative Writing
sample: Some people argue that social media is harmful for students because it wastes time and distracts them from revision. This is true when students use it without control. However, others believe that social media can be useful for learning, communication, and sharing educational videos. In my view, the problem is not social media itself, but the way students use it. If they manage their time and follow reliable pages, social media can support their studies.
commonMistakes: Only giving one side when the topic asks for debate
checklist: I checked that my conclusion answers the question
